name: Ph.D in Business Data Science

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The program has a clear doctoral research spine (BDS 791 Doctoral Seminar, BDS 792B Pre-Doctoral Research, BDS 725/726 Independent Study I-II, plus dissertation) wrapped around a strong methodological core spanning CS 634 Data Mining, CS 732 Advanced Machine Learning, DS 675 Machine Learning, DS 677 Deep Learning, and ECE 744 Optimization for Data Engineering, cohering well as a business-plus-data-science doctorate. The main weakness is scale: with 87 referenced courses and requirementGroups=0, the required core is not delineated from a very large elective menu spanning CS, DS, ECE, ECON, EM, FIN, and IE.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish a plan of study naming the required methods core, the business-domain breadth requirement, and the research/dissertation sequence, separating them from the large elective catalog so the doctoral pathway is legible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The deepestPrereqChain of 3 is healthy for a doctorate and shows genuine sequencing (FIN 600 to FIN 616 to FIN 620; CS 634 to CS 732), but missingCourseCount is 7 with dangling references including the dissertation code BDS 790A plus undergraduate codes (CS 280, MATH 211, MATH 333, MGMT 216, MGMT 492) and INTD 799 that do not resolve in the catalog. The undergraduate references are defensible as background, but a dangling dissertation code is a real catalog defect.
dimensions.suggestion: Resolve the seven dangling references, especially BDS 790A and INTD 799, and mark clearly which are undergraduate background assumptions versus in-program requirements.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or doctoral competencies are stated in the data, so the research capabilities the Ph.D. certifies (original research, methodological rigor, publication) are left implicit.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish doctoral program outcomes describing expected competencies in research design, advanced data-science methods, and independent scholarship.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null with no parsed plan and creditReconcileDelta uncomputable, so the coursework-versus-dissertation credit balance of a doctoral program (typically 75+ credits with a defined dissertation minimum) is not transparent; note BDS 791 correctly carries 0 credits as a seminar.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total doctoral credit requirement and the split among required coursework, qualifying/pre-doctoral research, and dissertation credits.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Most courses carry rich, specific descriptions, but a few key doctoral entries are thin or empty (BDS 791 Doctoral Seminar has an empty description; ECE 788 Selected Topics is generic), and the absence of a parsed plan of study means the 87-course menu is presented without indicating which courses fulfill which doctoral requirement.
dimensions.suggestion: Fill in the empty/generic descriptions for the doctoral seminar and research courses and group the catalog into required-core versus elective sections.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The methods coverage is at the current data-science frontier: DS 677 Deep Learning, CS 732 Advanced Machine Learning, CS 782 Pattern Recognition, CS 644 Introduction to Big Data, ECE 744 Optimization for Data Engineering, and FIN 616/620 data-driven financial analytics give doctoral students genuinely marketable, contemporary skills paired with business context.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a dedicated NLP/large-language-model course and a causal-inference course so the doctorate covers the generative-AI and causal-ML directions now central to business research.

descriptionQuality.justification: The large majority of the 87 courses have detailed, specific descriptions (CS 644 on big-data storage-to-analytics pipelines, ECE 744 on optimization for data engineering, FIN 620 on production data-driven financial applications); only a handful are thin or empty (BDS 791, ECE 788), which slightly lowers an otherwise strong aggregate.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are generally explicit and sensibly conditioned (CS 732 after CS 634 or CS 670; the FIN 600 to 616 to 620 chain), but missingCourseCount=7 means several stated prerequisites and the dissertation reference point to codes absent from the catalog, creating ambiguity about what students must actually complete.

missingTopics.topic: Causal inference and econometrics for research
missingTopics.rationale: The methods core is prediction-heavy (CS 634, DS 675/677, CS 732) but lacks an explicit causal-inference/econometrics course, essential for publishable business-research claims beyond correlation.

missingTopics.topic: Doctoral research methods and scholarly writing
missingTopics.rationale: Beyond FIN 780 (financial-research methods) there is no general research-design and publication course, a core need for a Ph.D. whose value is original scholarship.

missingTopics.topic: Natural language processing and large language models
missingTopics.rationale: Coverage spans classical ML and deep learning but no dedicated NLP/LLM course, despite text and generative models now dominating business data-science research.
CS 644 and ECE 744 touch scale and optimization, but reproducible-research practice and productionizing models are absent, leaving a gap between research prototypes and deployable science.
A data-science doctorate should require training in algorithmic fairness, data privacy, and responsible-AI governance; no such course appears in the referenced set.

emergingTrends.rationale: Extending DS 677 Deep Learning toward transformer architectures and LLM fine-tuning would put the program on the dominant current research trajectory.
Combining the strong ML core with causal methods answers the business-research demand for interventional, decision-relevant conclusions.

emergingTrends.trend: Reproducible and open science / ML systems
emergingTrends.rationale: Doctoral output increasingly requires reproducible pipelines and shared artifacts; a course building on CS 644 would prepare students for this norm.
Data-privacy law and AI regulation are reshaping what business data scientists may build, making governance a research and career necessity.
High-stakes business decisions demand interpretable models; a course on explainability would complement the pattern-recognition and deep-learning offerings (CS 782, DS 677).

topSuggestions: Publish a plan of study separating the required methods core, business-domain breadth, and research/dissertation sequence from the 87-course elective menu.
Resolve the seven dangling course references, prioritizing the dissertation code BDS 790A and INTD 799.
Add causal inference/econometrics and a general doctoral research-methods course to balance the prediction-heavy methods core.
Introduce an NLP/large-language-model course and an explainable/responsible-AI course to reach the current data-science frontier.
State total doctoral credits and the coursework-versus-dissertation split, and publish doctoral learning outcomes.
